presentation.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
presentation.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
presentation.author: AI Workbench Local

executive_summary:
Executive Comparison:  
The Access Review Evidence Log (EVD-101) is a synthetic, testing-focused record of Q1 2024 vendor-control evidence, highlighting missing or incomplete items that directly affect control closure timelines. In contrast, the Privileged Account Approval Email confirms a limited, conditional grant of standing privileged access to two Northwind administrators for a pilot, with strict operational and review controls. The key business difference is that the Evidence Log identifies gaps requiring remediation to close a control, while the Approval Email authorizes specific, tightly scoped access under renewal conditions.

- Evidence Log flags missing/incomplete items impacting control closure; Approval Email grants conditional access contingent on quarterly renewal.  
- Evidence Log is synthetic and for testing; Approval Email is a real authorization with operational constraints.  
- Evidence Log lists artifacts with statuses and owners; Approval Email specifies scope (ticketed support/break-fix) and verification requirements.  
- Evidence Log addresses control closure timelines; Approval Email emphasizes personnel verification before each quarterly renewal.  
- Evidence Log pertains to vendor-control evidence; Approval Email pertains to privileged access governance for support roles.  
- Evidence Log requires resolution of deficiencies; Approval Email enforces strict access limitations and logging.
